{{Short description="Theoretical gas composed of randomly moving, non-interacting point particles}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Ideal gas
| image = Gas molecules.gif
| caption = Simulation of ideal gas molecules undergoing elastic collisions in a container.
| topics = [[Thermodynamics]], [[Kinetic theory of gases]], [[Ideal gas law]], [[Statistical mechanics]]
}}

An '''ideal gas''' is a theoretical [[gas]] composed of many randomly moving point particles whose only interactions are perfectly [[elastic collision]]s. The ideal gas concept is useful because it obeys a simplified [[equation of state]], the [[ideal gas law]], and is amenable to analysis under [[statistical mechanics]].

== Ideal gas law ==
{{Main|Ideal gas law}}
:<math>PV = nRT = Nk_B T</math>

where:
* <math>P</math> is [[pressure]]
* <math>V</math> is [[volume]]
* <math>n</math> is number of moles
* <math>R</math> is the [[molar gas constant]]
* <math>N</math> is number of molecules
* <math>k_B</math> is the [[Boltzmann constant]]
* <math>T</math> is [[temperature]]

== Assumptions ==
The ideal gas model assumes:
# Gas molecules are point particles with zero volume
# No intermolecular forces except during elastic collisions
# Collisions are perfectly elastic (kinetic energy conserved)
# Random motion in all directions
# Large number of particles (statistical treatment valid)

== Kinetic theory derivation ==
{{Main|Kinetic theory of gases}}
From the kinetic theory, the pressure arises from molecular impacts on container walls:
:<math>P = \frac{1}{3}\frac{Nm\langle v^2 \rangle}{V}</math>

Combining with <math>\langle E_k \rangle = \frac{3}{2}k_B T</math>:
:<math>PV = Nk_B T</math>

== Key results ==
* [[Kinetic energy]] per molecule: <math>\frac{3}{2}k_B T</math>
* [[Root mean square speed]]: <math>v_{\text{rms}} = \sqrt{\frac{3k_B T}{m}} = \sqrt{\frac{3RT}{M}}</math>
* [[Internal energy]] depends only on temperature: <math>U = \frac{3}{2}nRT</math> (monatomic)
* [[Heat capacity]] at constant volume: <math>C_V = \frac{3}{2}R</math> (monatomic)

== Deviations from ideality ==
Real gases deviate from ideal behaviour due to:
* Finite molecular volume (significant at high pressure)
* [[Intermolecular force]]s (significant at low temperature)

The [[van der Waals equation]] corrects for these:
:<math>(P + a\frac{n^2}{V^2})(V - nb) = nRT</math>

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Boyle's law]]
* [[Ideal gas law]]
* [[Kinetic theory of gases]]
* [[Thermodynamics]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Ideal gas| ]]
[[Category:Thermodynamics]]
[[Category:Statistical mechanics]]
